prompt: A black-and-white manga comic panel in a clean seinen/anime style, showing 4 girls from a rock band sitting side by side in a cramped music studio right after an electrical accident, with a chaotic body-swap theme. Large hand-lettered title text at the top reads BODY SWAP PANIC!. The scene is framed as a single wide panel with dramatic speed lines and comic energy. From left to right: girl 1 is a short-haired shy girl in a black band T-shirt and dark pants, staring at her hand and speaking in shock, with a speech bubble that says EH?! MY VOICE!?; girl 2 is a calm, expressionless blonde girl with long straight hair, wearing a black band T-shirt, scarf, and plaid pants, sitting with one hand on her hip and saying in a smaller speech bubble, "Hmm. This is... loud."; girl 3 is a flustered cheerful girl with medium-length hair in a black band T-shirt and long skirt, blushing, sweating, clutching her hands near her mouth, with a large speech bubble that says H-H-HEEEEH?! I'M KITA-CHAN?!; girl 4 is a long-haired girl in a zip hoodie and skirt, sitting rigidly with arms crossed and eyes squeezed shut, complaining in a tall speech bubble that says BOCCHI-CHAN'S BODY IS SO... CRAMPED!. All 4 girls have small circular inset head icons above or near them showing whose mind is inside whose body, for a body-swap explanation device. The band shirts have a simple Japanese logo on the chest. Background shows exactly 3 visible studio elements: a drum kit in the back, an amplifier or speaker cabinet, and cables or equipment edges around the floor and sides. Use monochrome screentone shading, halftone textures, blush marks, sweat drops, bold ink outlines, and expressive manga lettering. The mood is comedic panic, awkward confusion, and post-shock chaos in a rehearsal room. Composition should feel crowded, readable, and like a fan comic inspired by Bocchi the Rock!.
